The search results show that Rivero's academic work focuses on theoretical physics topics. His viXra publications address string theory and particle physics, specifically dealing with self-referencing postulates in Chan-Paton factors and the SO(32) landscape.

Based on the search results, I found information about these three topics:

**viXra**: viXra.org is an e-print archive launched in 2009 as an alternative to the popular arXiv.org service owned by Cornell University. It was founded by scientists who found that they were unable to submit their articles to arXiv.org because of Cornell University's policy of endorsements and moderation designed to filter out e-prints which were considered inappropriate.

**Alejandro Rivero**: Alejandro Rivero is a physicist who has contributed work to viXra. One of his ideas is the "sBootstrap" which aims to derive all the fermions of the standard model, as fermionic superpartners of mesons and diquarks made of the five light flavors of quark.

**sBootstrap**: The "sBootstrap" is an idea that aims to derive all the fermions of the standard model, as fermionic superpartners of mesons and diquarks made of the five light flavors of quark.

content.content:
# Paper Details

**Title:** "Up to So(32) Via Supersymmetry "Bootstrap""

**Author:** Alejandro Rivero

**Submission Date:** January 6, 2019

**viXra ID:** 1901.0074

**Category:** High Energy Particle Physics

**Abstract:** The paper presents "a simple self-referencing postulate in the Chan-Paton factors of a string" that aims to constrain aspects of SO(32) model development with potential connections to standard model physics.

**Format:** The submission comprises three similar versions of 3-4 pages each, plus a concluding section

**Download:** Available as PDF (v1)

**Engagement:** The paper has received 199 unique-IP document downloads

**Note:** As hosted on viXra (a preprint repository), this work has not undergone peer review and should be considered preliminary.

# Paper Details

**Title:** "Possible Sensibility of Nuclear Fragmentation to the Mass of W"

**Author:** Alejandro Rivero

**Date:** September 7, 2015

**Identifier:** viXra:1509.0090

**Abstract:** The researcher examines "the possibility of an enhancement of the yields of asymmetric fission when the mass of one of the fragments has the same size that the electroweak W mass" (98 characters), potentially indicating a recoil resonance effect.

**Additional Information:**
- **Document Type:** 6-page draft note with graphical updates
- **Downloads:** 182 unique-IP downloads
- **Repository:** viXra.org (pre-print archive, not peer-reviewed)
- **PDF Link:** Available at vixra.org

**Note:** This submission builds upon the author's previous work from 2004, exploring connections between nuclear physics phenomena and electroweak parameters.

# Paper Details: Bootstrapping Generations

**Title:** Bootstrapping Generations

**viXra ID:** 1408.0196

**Author:** A. Rivero

**Publication Date:** August 1, 2014

**Submission History:**
- Version 1: August 28, 2014
- Version 2: August 29, 2014

**Category:** High Energy Particle Physics

**Abstract:** "A supersymmetric version of Chew's 'democratic bootstrap' argument predicts the existence of three generations of particles, with a quark, of type 'up', more massive that the other five."

**Length:** 4 pages

**Downloads:** 323 unique-IP document downloads

**Available Format:** PDF (both v1 and v2)

**Repository Note:** The paper is hosted on viXra.org, which is a preprint repository rather than a peer-reviewed journal, so findings should be considered preliminary.

# Paper Details: "Folding a Pattern"

**Title:** Folding a Pattern

**Author:** Alejandro Rivero

**viXra ID:** 1302.0006

**Submission Date:** February 1, 2013 (v1); February 15, 2013 (v2)

**Category:** High Energy Particle Physics

**Length:** 4 pages

**Abstract:** The author proposes reorganizing the standard model and its mesons to "build supersymmetric multiplets," noting the presentation invites further refinement in selecting candidates for recombination.

**PDF Link:** Available as v2 (latest version)

**Metrics:** The paper has been downloaded 307 times (unique IP addresses)

**Note:** viXra is a preprint repository, and papers may lack peer-review verification before publication.

# Paper Details

**Title:** "A New Koide Triplet: Strange, Charm, Bottom."

**Author:** Alejandro Rivero

**Date:** November 15, 2011 (v1); November 28, 2011 (v2)

**viXra ID:** 1111.0062

**Abstract:** The researcher demonstrates that strange, charm, and bottom quarks form a Koide tuple when using a negative sign for √m_s. This extends a previously identified c-b-t tuple and exhibits near-orthogonality to the original charged lepton triplet.

**Length:** 5 pages

**Downloads:** 780 unique-IP downloads

**Document Type:** Pre-print (not peer-reviewed)

**PDF Link:** Available at vixra.org/pdf/1111.0062v2.pdf

# Paper Details

**Title:** "No Elementary Scalars in Experimental Supersymmetry"

**Author:** Alejandro Rivero

**viXra ID:** 1102.0034

**Submission Date:** February 20, 2011

**Length:** 2 pages

**Download:** PDF available

**Abstract Summary:**
The paper presents an unconventional interpretation of SU(5) symmetry found in scalar particle spectra within the Supersymmetric Standard Model. The author proposes that these scalars may represent different manifestations of the QCD string rather than independent particles. Based on this hypothesis, only gauginos and possibly two neutral Higgs particles would be viable candidates for detection at the LHC.

**Document Downloads:** 280 unique-IP downloads

**Repository Note:** viXra.org is a preprint archive, not a peer-reviewed journal, so papers may lack formal verification before publication.

Based on the search results, I found information about Alejandro Rivero and his work on the sBootstrap approach:

Alejandro Rivero is a theoretical physics PhD from Zaragoza University, and he works at BIFI - universidad de zaragoza and has research interests in social networks, big data, and particle physics.

Regarding his sBootstrap work, his predictions depend heavily on not linking all the quarks to strings, and the matching of bosons with fermions predicts that you can only link five kinds of quarks. A supersymmetric version of Chew's old democratic bootstrap argument predicts the existence of three generations of quarks and leptons with one quark, of type "up", more massive than the other five.

The search results also indicate he has proposed an interpretation for the adjoint representation of the SO(32) group to classify the scalars of a generic Supersymmetric Standard Model having just three generations of particles, via a flavour group SU(5), showing that this same interpretation arises from a simple postulate of self-consistence of composites for these scalars.

Rivero has published multiple preprints on arXiv related to particle physics, including work on Koide formulas and aspects of the standard model.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
I now have a comprehensive picture. Here is the complete list of viXra papers by Alejandro Rivero.

---

## viXra Papers by Alejandro Rivero

All papers are listed on the viXra author page at [https://vixra.org/author/alejandro_rivero](https://vixra.org/author/alejandro_rivero). Here are the six papers found, in reverse chronological order:

### 1. "Up to SO(32) Via Supersymmetry 'Bootstrap'"
- **viXra ID:** [1901.0074](https://vixra.org/abs/1901.0074)
- **Year:** 2019 (submitted January 6, 2019)
- **Pages:** ~3-4 pages (multiple similar versions plus a concluding section)
- **Category:** High Energy Particle Physics
- **Summary:** Presents a self-referencing postulate in the Chan-Paton factors of a string to constrain aspects of the SO(32) model, with connections to standard model physics. Proposes an interpretation of the adjoint representation of SO(32) to classify the scalars of a generic Supersymmetric Standard Model with three generations, via a flavour group SU(5).
- **Downloads:** 199

### 2. "Possible Sensibility of Nuclear Fragmentation to the Mass of W"
- **viXra ID:** [1509.0090](https://vixra.org/abs/1509.0090)
- **Year:** 2015 (submitted September 7, 2015)
- **Pages:** 6 pages (draft note with graphical updates)
- **Category:** High Energy Particle Physics
- **Summary:** Examines the possibility of an enhancement of the yields of asymmetric fission when the mass of one of the fragments has the same size as the electroweak W mass (~80 GeV), potentially indicating a recoil resonance effect. Builds on earlier work from 2004.
- **Downloads:** 182

### 3. "Bootstrapping Generations"
- **viXra ID:** [1408.0196](https://vixra.org/abs/1408.0196)
- **Year:** 2014 (submitted August 28, 2014; replaced August 29, 2014)
- **Pages:** 4 pages
- **Category:** High Energy Particle Physics
- **Summary:** A supersymmetric version of Chew's "democratic bootstrap" argument predicts the existence of three generations of particles, with a quark of type "up" more massive than the other five. This is a core sBootstrap paper.
- **Downloads:** 323

### 4. "Folding a Pattern"
- **viXra ID:** [1302.0006](https://vixra.org/abs/1302.0006)
- **Year:** 2013 (submitted February 1, 2013; replaced February 15, 2013)
- **Pages:** 4 pages
- **Category:** High Energy Particle Physics
- **Summary:** Proposes reorganizing the standard model particles and their mesons to build supersymmetric multiplets, exploring candidate selections for recombination.
- **Downloads:** 307

### 5. "A New Koide Triplet: Strange, Charm, Bottom"
- **viXra ID:** [1111.0062](https://vixra.org/abs/1111.0062)
- **Year:** 2011 (submitted November 15, 2011; replaced November 28, 2011)
- **Pages:** 5 pages
- **Category:** High Energy Particle Physics
- **Summary:** Demonstrates that strange, charm, and bottom quarks form a Koide tuple when using a negative sign for the square root of the strange quark mass. This extends a previously identified c-b-t tuple and exhibits near-orthogonality to the original charged lepton triplet.
- **Downloads:** 780 (most downloaded of all six papers)

### 6. "No Elementary Scalars in Experimental Supersymmetry"
- **viXra ID:** [1102.0034](https://vixra.org/abs/1102.0034)
- **Year:** 2011 (submitted February 20, 2011)
- **Pages:** 2 pages
- **Category:** High Energy Particle Physics
- **Summary:** Presents an unconventional interpretation of SU(5) symmetry found in scalar particle spectra within the Supersymmetric Standard Model. Proposes that these scalars may represent different manifestations of the QCD string rather than independent particles, meaning only gauginos and possibly two neutral Higgs particles would be viable LHC candidates.
- **Downloads:** 280

---

### Summary Table

| # | viXra ID | Title | Year | Pages |
|---|----------|-------|------|-------|
| 1 | 1901.0074 | Up to SO(32) Via Supersymmetry "Bootstrap" | 2019 | 3-4 |
| 2 | 1509.0090 | Possible Sensibility of Nuclear Fragmentation to the Mass of W | 2015 | 6 |
| 3 | 1408.0196 | Bootstrapping Generations | 2014 | 4 |
| 4 | 1302.0006 | Folding a Pattern | 2013 | 4 |
| 5 | 1111.0062 | A New Koide Triplet: Strange, Charm, Bottom | 2011 | 5 |
| 6 | 1102.0034 | No Elementary Scalars in Experimental Supersymmetry | 2011 | 2 |

**Total: 6 viXra papers** spanning 2011 to 2019, all in the High Energy Particle Physics category. The papers collectively trace the development of the sBootstrap idea -- from the initial observation about scalar particles and SU(5) symmetry (2011), through the Koide mass formula extensions (2011), pattern-folding of supersymmetric multiplets (2013), the democratic bootstrap argument for three generations (2014), a nuclear physics observation (2015), and finally the connection to SO(32) string theory (2019).

Sources:
- [viXra author page: Alejandro Rivero](https://vixra.org/author/alejandro_rivero)
- [Guest post: Alejandro Rivero, "sBootstrap" - A Quantum Diaries Survivor](https://dorigo.wordpress.com/2007/10/16/guest-post-alejandro-rivero-sbootstrap/)
- [Alejandro Rivero - Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=D9BviOkAAAAJ&hl=en)
- [Alejandro Rivero - Academia.edu](https://unizar.academia.edu/arivero)
- [Alejandro Rivero - ResearchGate](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Alejandro-Rivero-3)
